text.title: aws-s3-storage Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for aws-s3-storage as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: S3 buckets with public read access enabled via bucket policy or ACL.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Public buckets are indexed by search engines and security scanners within minutes of creation. Even accidentally exposed sensitive files become permanently cached and accessible.

failure-mode.description: Buckets without a lifecycle rule to abort incomplete multipart uploads.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multipart uploads that are started but never completed remain as invisible partial uploads charged at standard S3 rates. A single misconfigured client can accumulate thousands of partial uploads in days.

failure-mode.description: S3 buckets in production without any lifecycle rules configured.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unmanaged buckets accumulate costs from three sources: incomplete multipart uploads, non-current versions (if versioned), and expired delete markers. A single active application can generate hundreds of GB of orphaned data per month.
